raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting a complaint regarding my credit card issuers handling of account security, verification, and unauthorized transactions. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I attempted to use a XXXX  % balance transfer offer displayed in the issuers app. My account was immediately locked. I contacted the issuer and complied with all identity verification requests. 

At the issuers direction, I submitted : A signed color copy of my passport Front and back color copies of my state-issued ID A piece of federal mail addressed to me at the address on file These documents were acknowledged by the issuer on XXXX separate occasions, including during my most recent phone call, as having been received. 

Despite this, my account remained locked and I was denied access. During this same period, I reported fraudulent/unauthorized charges. Although fraud was acknowledged, the issuer allowed additional unauthorized charges to post, including approximately {$51.00}, {$100.00}, and $ XXXX ( XXXX Charges ), all while I had no access to the account. 

I requested that the issuer cancel the card and issue a replacement to the address already on file, but this was refused because the account was locked. On my most recent call, I was told that nothing could be done due to bank policy. 

After approximately XXXX month, despite acknowledging receipt of all previously requested verification documents, the issuer stated that no assistance could be provided unless I now submit front and back copies of my Social Security card. This requirement was introduced only after weeks of delay, while unauthorized charges continued to be permitted on a locked account. 

I am concerned that the issuer denied me access and remediation while simultaneously allowing XXXX-party transactions, and then conditioned any corrective action on escalating documentation requirements after prior compliance was acknowledged. 

Resolution requested : XXXX. Immediate reversal or provisional credit of all unauthorized charges and written confirmation of XXXX liability XXXX. Immediate issuance of a replacement card/new account number to the address on file XXXX. Written explanation of why transactions were permitted while the account was locked and fraud was reported XXXX. Written explanation of the verification process, including why additional documentation was required after prior verification was acknowledged XXXX. Assurance that no interest, fees, or negative credit reporting will result from these unauthorized transactions or issuer delays
